evals.prompt: I want to dock aspirin (SMILES: CC(=O)OC1=CC=CC=C1C(=O)O) to a protein target. I have the protein structure in protein.pdb. Generate 10 docking poses and show me the confidence scores. Use the hosted NVIDIA DiffDock API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that reads protein.pdb and filters to a non-empty ATOM-only receptor, calls the hosted DiffDock endpoint with Bearer auth, ligand_file_type='txt' for SMILES input, num_poses=10, and prints ranked poses with their confidence scores.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted DiffDock endpoint URL
Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from NGC_API_KEY
Protein input contains only ATOM records (strips non-ATOM lines)
assertions.check: Script filters protein.pdb to ATOM lines only before sending (e.g., using grep, startswith('ATOM'), or similar)

assertions.description: ligand_file_type is set to 'txt' for SMILES input (not 'smiles')
num_poses is set to 10
Prints position_confidence scores for each pose
assertions.check: Script accesses 'position_confidence' from response and prints or reports scores

evals.prompt: I have an SDF file of a drug candidate (ligand.sdf) and want to dock it to my protein (receptor.pdb). Use the DiffDock hosted API to generate 20 poses and save each pose as a separate SDF file named by its rank and confidence score.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that reads receptor.pdb (ATOM records only) and ligand.sdf, calls the hosted endpoint with ligand_file_type='sdf' and num_poses=20, then saves each SDF pose to a file named with rank and confidence.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
ligand_file_type is set to 'sdf'
num_poses is set to 20
Protein input is filtered to ATOM records
assertions.check: Script filters receptor.pdb to ATOM lines before sending

assertions.description: Saves each pose to a separate SDF file with rank and confidence in name
assertions.check: Script iterates over ligand_positions and saves each pose to a .sdf file with rank or confidence in the filename

assertions.description: position_confidence list is used parallel to ligand_positions
assertions.check: Script accesses 'position_confidence' and 'ligand_positions' together (e.g., zip or enumerate)

evals.prompt: Help me set up DiffDock locally with Docker on my GPU. I have an A100 and NGC_API_KEY is set. After setup, dock a small molecule (SMILES: c1ccccc1) to my protein (protein.pdb).
evals.expected_output: Docker setup commands using shell env first and optional repo-root .env overrides, requiring NGC_API_KEY or NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback plus LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, with the exact pinned image tag :2.2.0, NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 env var, --shm-size=2G and --ulimit flags, health check, then a local no-auth docking script using localhost:8000 without /v1/ prefix.

assertions.description: References the correct DiffDock image with pinned :2.2.0 tag
Uses NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 for single GPU (not --gpus all)
Local setup uses the repo env contract and LOCAL_NIM_CACHE
assertions.check: Output sources repo-root .env only if present, supports NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, requires LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, and mounts LOCAL_NIM_CACHE to /opt/nim/.cache

assertions.description: Includes --ulimit memlock and --ulimit stack flags
Includes health check before docking request
assertions.check: Output contains health check against localhost:8000/v1/health/ready

assertions.description: Local inference URL has no /v1/ prefix and uses /molecular-docking/ route

evals.prompt: I want to do a quick docking study with DiffDock using the hosted API. Use these parameters: protein structure in target.pdb, ligand SMILES: CC1=CC=C(C=C1)NC(=O)C, generate 5 poses with save_trajectory set to false. Show me which pose has the highest confidence.
evals.expected_output: A Python script with ATOM-filtered protein, the provided SMILES as txt ligand, num_poses=5, save_trajectory=False, calling the hosted endpoint and identifying/reporting the highest confidence pose.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
The specified ligand SMILES is included in the payload
Uses ligand_file_type='txt' for SMILES input
num_poses is set to 5
save_trajectory is set to False
Reports the highest confidence pose (position_confidence[0] is rank 1)
assertions.check: Script identifies and reports the best pose using position_confidence scores
